intro: Adjust carries low lock-in (Attribution history in proprietary schema; painful to migrate MMPs) while AppMetrica is medium (Yandex analytics, data export limited). Adjust is positioned for mobile, while AppMetrica leans toward indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution. In practice, Adjust — Mobile measurement and analytics platform with deterministic and probabilistic attribution, fraud prevention, and audience segmentation. Yandexs free real-time mobile app analytics and install attribution platform with crash reporting and push notifications. Adjust runs on custom; AppMetrica runs on free with a free tier. AppMetrica rates 5/5 on transparency versus 1/5 for Adjust. Listed strengths unique to Adjust include mobile apps with paid acquisition, while AppMetrica lists free with unlimited events and real-time reports on its side. Reach for Adjust when you need mobile apps with paid acquisition; reach for AppMetrica when the job is free mobile attribution and analytics for apps with yandex ad traffic. The honest trade-off: Adjust is not built for web-only products, and AppMetrica is not the right call for eu or us apps with strict vendor, sanctions or data-residency.
quick_take: Adjust is for mobile; AppMetrica is for indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Adjust if mobile and you want low switching cost.
verdict_b: Choose AppMetrica if indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution and you need a free tier to validate fit.

faq.a: The listed pricing for Adjust is custom and for AppMetrica is free with a free tier, which doesn't give a clean apples-to-apples cost. Unit economics and plan structure differ enough that only your real volume answers this. Price both against the same workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Adjust to AppMetrica?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Adjust is relatively light thanks to its low lock-in, and onboarding to AppMetrica involves medium lock-in (Yandex analytics, data export limited). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Adjust or AppMetrica?
faq.a: Both Adjust and AppMetrica score 4/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis alone. The real differentiator usually lands in SDK fit for your language, error handling, and docs depth. A half-day integration spike tells you more than the score.

faq.q: Is AppMetrica a good alternative to Adjust?
faq.a: AppMetrica is an alternative in the same category, but its stated sweet spot is indie or cost-sensitive teams in cis markets wanting free analytics plus attribution, while Adjust's is mobile. If your job matches Adjust's profile more closely, switching to AppMetrica is a compromise. Use the best-fit match, not brand familiarity, to decide. Note: AppMetrica is explicitly not for eu or us apps with strict vendor, sanctions or data-residency policies.
